macro_events.event_summary: UAE reports being under attack by Iranian missiles and drones despite a ceasefire, escalating Persian Gulf conflict risk.
primary_drivers: Gulf oil supply/shipping disruption risk drives crude prices higher
Defense procurement demand surge from Middle East allies
Safe-haven flows into gold and defensive assets

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with significant upstream leverage; direct beneficiary of Brent spike from Gulf supply risk premium. Strait of Hormuz carries ~20% of global oil flows.
High-beta US E&P with strong leverage to crude price spikes; historically outperforms majors on geopolitical oil shocks.
Maker of THAAD and PAC-3 missile defense systems already deployed in UAE; direct restock/expansion demand if interceptors are being expended against Iranian missiles/drones.
Patriot missile manufacturer with active UAE contracts; interceptor consumption against drone/missile attacks drives near-term replenishment orders.
Global Hawk and integrated air/missile defense systems supplier; benefits from increased Gulf air defense procurement.
Pure-play counter-drone and target drone systems; directly relevant as UAE faces drone swarm attacks driving counter-UAS demand.
Permian-focused pure-play E&P with high oil-price torque; benefits from sustained crude premium without international asset risk.

losers.rationale: Major operator of long-haul routes through/near Gulf airspace; jet fuel cost spike plus potential rerouting/airspace closures hit margins. Middle East overflight disruptions raise costs.
International exposure including Gulf hub connectivity; jet fuel is largest variable cost and crude spike directly compresses margins.
Highly leveraged airline with thin margins; most sensitive to fuel cost shock among US carriers.
Cruise operator with Middle East/Red Sea itineraries already disrupted; further escalation forces cancellations and bunker fuel cost rises.
Travel demand softens on geopolitical fear; Middle East booking exposure plus broader risk-off travel sentiment.
Low-income consumer discretionary squeezed by gasoline price passthrough from crude spike; gas prices are the most visible inflation indicator for core customer.

headlines: UAE says it's under attack from Iranian missiles and drones despite ceasefire

macro_events.event_summary: A compromise version of the Clarity Act preserves stablecoin yield/rewards programs, sending Circle (USDC issuer) up 16%.
primary_drivers: Regulatory clarity removes existential overhang on stablecoin business model
Preserved rewards/yield mechanism keeps USDC competitive vs bank deposits and Treasuries-only stablecoins
Validation of crypto-native payment rails increases addressable market for issuers and distributors

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct subject of headline — USDC issuer whose core revenue model (reserve interest + rewards-driven distribution) is preserved by the compromise. Removes binary regulatory risk.

Coinbase has a revenue-sharing agreement with Circle on USDC reserve interest (~50% split on USDC held on platform). Stablecoin revenue is one of Coinbase's largest non-trading revenue lines; preserved rewards directly increases this stream.

Robinhood has been expanding USDC rewards/yield to retail users and is part of the Global Dollar Network stablecoin consortium. Clarity on rewards directly enables their stablecoin yield product roadmap.
Broad crypto regulatory de-risking lifts the entire digital asset complex; Strategy is the highest-beta liquid crypto proxy on US exchanges and trades on regulatory sentiment.
Issues PYUSD stablecoin via Paxos; preserved rewards framework legitimizes their consumer-facing stablecoin strategy and the PayPal/Venmo yield offerings.
Galaxy Digital has direct stablecoin/crypto infrastructure exposure and benefits from any improvement in US crypto regulatory clarity that drives institutional adoption.

losers.rationale: Stablecoin rewards programs paying ~4-5% on dollar balances directly compete with brokerage cash sweep economics; preservation of the rewards model is a structural negative for cash-sweep net interest income at brokerages. Schwab is most exposed given heavy reliance on NII from idle cash.
Online banks competing for retail dollar deposits face a more entrenched competitor in yield-bearing stablecoins now that the rewards mechanism survives legislation.

headlines: Circle jumps 16% on Clarity Act compromise that preserves stablecoin rewards

macro_events.event_summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang publicly states Nvidia now has zero market share in China, confirming complete loss of the Chinese AI chip market due to US export controls.
primary_drivers: US export controls fully shutting Nvidia out of China AI accelerator market
Domestic Chinese chip designers filling the vacuum
Revenue headwind to Nvidia partially offset by oversubscribed non-China demand

beneficiaries.rationale: Not a direct beneficiary in China (same export rules) but benefits competitively ex-China: if Nvidia's TAM is now structurally smaller, AMD's MI300/MI350 share gains in US/EU hyperscalers look relatively more valuable, and any Nvidia weakness drives rotation within AI-accelerator trade.
Custom ASIC partner to Google/Meta; Nvidia's lost China revenue reinforces the narrative that hyperscaler custom silicon (TPU, MTIA via Broadcom) is the structural share-taker against merchant GPUs.

Server OEM whose China exposure was already curtailed; with Nvidia confirming zero China share, US/EU AI server demand remains the entire pie and SMCI keeps its allocation without China-diversion risk weighing on supply.

losers.rationale: CEO explicitly confirms 0% China share — China was historically ~20-25% of data center revenue pre-controls and management had still been guiding to some H20 recovery. Zero confirms a permanent loss of a multi-billion-dollar revenue stream and removes any optionality on license relief.
Nvidia is TSMC's largest or second-largest customer; permanent loss of China demand caps Nvidia wafer orders at the margin (CoWoS allocations for China-spec parts go to zero).

Reduced Nvidia China-spec volume plus accelerated Chinese domestic foundry/AI-chip self-sufficiency narrative reinforces that China is a closed market for Western leading-edge tools — pressures long-term WFE TAM assumptions.
Nvidia is a key Arm licensee for Grace CPU; lost China GPU+CPU systems revenue reduces royalty pull-through, and Chinese self-sufficiency push favors RISC-V over Arm at the margin.

Vertiv's AI data center cooling/power orderbook is heavily tied to Nvidia GPU deployment cadence; any narrative crack in Nvidia growth (China = 0) drives high-beta sympathy selling in AI infrastructure picks-and-shovels.

headlines: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says company now has zero market share in China
